questions: What is the correct approach for making --rating optional when --reply-to is present?
Does the CLI need any changes for the LOVE icon update or the nullable rating server change?

answers.answer: In comments-post.ts, restructure the rating flag handling: (1) Check for --reply-to first using getFlag (already at line 36). (2) If --reply-to IS present, use getFlag (optional) for --rating instead of requireFlag. (3) If --reply-to is NOT present (top-level post), keep requireFlag for --rating as current behavior. (4) When --reply-to is present and no --rating provided, omit rating from the POST body entirely (server accepts null rating for replies). (5) When --reply-to is present and --rating IS provided, include it normally. The body construction at lines 42-47 already conditionally adds fields, so omitting rating when it's undefined will naturally exclude it from the JSON body.
evidence: comments-post.ts:29 — requireFlag(args, '--rating', ...) currently always requires rating
comments-post.ts:36 — const replyTo = getFlag(args, '--reply-to') already reads the flag
comments-post.ts:42-47 — body construction: 'const body = { anchor: section, rating }; if (content) body.content = content; if (replyTo) body.parentCommentId = replyTo'
Server library-comment-service.ts:69-73 — validation only enforces rating for top-level

answers.answer: No functional changes needed. The CLI uses 'love' as the flag value which maps to 'LOVE' stored value — this is purely a data mapping and is unaffected by the client-side icon change. For the nullable rating server change, the body construction at lines 42-47 only adds fields that are present — when rating is undefined (reply without rating), it won't be included in the JSON body, which the server correctly interprets as null. SKILL.md description of rating values ('thumbs-up (up), thumbs-down (down), love') is correct and needs no update — 'love' is the CLI concept, the icon is a UI concept.
evidence: comments-post.ts:42-47 — body adds fields conditionally; undefined rating won't be serialized
SKILL.md:170 — 'Rating values: thumbs-up (up), thumbs-down (down), love' (correct as-is)

statement: CLI needs 1 targeted fix: make --rating conditional in comments-post.ts. Read --reply-to first, then use getFlag (optional) instead of requireFlag (mandatory) for --rating when replying. When no --reply-to, keep requireFlag. No other CLI changes needed for the icon or nullable rating updates.
